expected_response: Edges are the connections that route state from one node to the next. After a node finishes, an edge decides where the journey continues — it guides the flow through the graph. In this tavern, every exit is an edge; the routing logic that carries you to the next encounter.

description: Mira explains LangQuest as the LangChain learning universe
input: ask her how the langquest world works
expected_response: LangQuest runs on LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith. LangGraph keeps track of the state, guiding it from one point to the next like a map through a forest of choices. LangSmith observes every path taken, helping you understand the decisions made. Mira smiles: you are already inside the graph.

description: Mira discusses goblin lore with prompt clarity
input: ask mira about the goblins you heard about
expected_response: The Prompt Goblin thrives on wasted potential — vague questions and imprecise prompts feed it. Every wasted token makes it stronger. Every clear, precise question weakens it. Mira lowers her voice: if you want to deal with it, you must be specific. Precision is the only thing that works against it.
